# Suspec

> Suspec is an opinionated methodology for working with coding agents — shipped as a globally installed skill family, backed by a deterministic checker. The skills structure the working artifacts around the code: the spec that states intent, the task split when one spec fans out, the review packet that reconciles evidence against the spec, the findings worth keeping. Plain markdown, any agent, and your repos take nothing.

Install: `npx skills add jcosta33/suspec-skills -g`. Artifacts live beside the agent's own native artifacts — the agent chooses the spot, every step names files by explicit full path, and nothing is committed to the adopter's repo. Durable lessons become native harness memories; decisions become ADRs; code stays king. The optional CLI (`suspec check`) is the honesty floor: deterministic checks a lazy or dishonest reviewer cannot fake — coverage complete, evidence commands match the spec, every Pass carries evidence, references resolve. Exit codes are the API: 0 clean, 1 warning, 2 blocking. It reports facts and never renders a review result; the human owns Pass/Fail. Three keys — intent, review, findings — are present on virtually every change, at whatever weight it earns; the rest (the spec, the task split, the inventory, the change plan, the checker) is scaffold the work pulls in when it earns it. Proportional rigor throughout: a trivial fix gets a one-line inline spec and no file; a feature gets a lean spec; no step exists for ceremony's sake, and every step keeps a by-hand path.

## Start here

- [What is Suspec?](https://suspecframework.dev/docs/01-what-is-suspec/): The methodology, shipped as skills — what it gives you and what it does not replace.
- [The basic workflow](https://suspecframework.dev/docs/02-basic-workflow/): The trivial inline path first, then the full loop — intent, spec, implement, review packet, check, findings.
- [Where files live](https://suspecframework.dev/docs/03-where-files-live/): Beside your native artifacts, by explicit path — no prescribed location, nothing in the adopter's repo.
- [Writing specs](https://suspecframework.dev/docs/04-writing-specs/): Requirements with AC ids and Verify with: lines an agent can work from.
- [Brownfield work and change plans](https://suspecframework.dev/docs/05-brownfield-and-change-plans/): Structural changes to existing code, planned before they sprawl.
- [Creating tasks](https://suspecframework.dev/docs/06-creating-tasks/): The task split — cut task packets only when one spec splits into parallel slices.
- [Running agents](https://suspecframework.dev/docs/07-running-agents/): Handing the spec — and the task, when split — by explicit path.
- [Reviewing agent output](https://suspecframework.dev/docs/08-reviewing-output/): An independent reviewer reconciles evidence per requirement; empty evidence is Unverified, never Pass.
- [Saving findings](https://suspecframework.dev/docs/09-saving-findings/): Durable lessons become native harness memories; ephemeral findings die with the packet.
- [Integrations](https://suspecframework.dev/docs/10-integrations/): The CLI, the MCP server, agent catalogs, issue trackers, PRs and CI — all optional.

## Tutorial

- [Your first loop: a hands-on walkthrough](https://suspecframework.dev/docs/tutorial/README/): Build one change end to end, the guided track.

## Worked examples

- [A bug fix](https://suspecframework.dev/docs/examples/bug-fix/): The short path on a small defect.
- [A feature from a ticket](https://suspecframework.dev/docs/examples/feature-from-ticket/): A vague ticket turned into a spec and a reviewed result.
- [A 41-file refactor, reviewed by exception](https://suspecframework.dev/docs/examples/large-pr-review/): The flagship demo — a large change reviewed without reading every line.

## Product pages

- [Full documentation, concatenated](https://suspecframework.dev/llms-full.txt): Every user-facing doc (the numbered path, the tutorial, and the worked examples) in one markdown file for full-context ingestion.
- [Documentation home](https://suspecframework.dev/docs/): The full documentation, including the reference layer and the decision ledger.
- [What is Suspec?](https://suspecframework.dev/what-is-suspec/): Direct product overview, boundaries, and non-goals.
- [The loop](https://suspecframework.dev/the-loop/): The trivial path, the full loop, the keys, and the scaffold.
- [Get started](https://suspecframework.dev/get-started/): Adopting is one install; nothing lands in your repo.

## Packages

- [suspec-skills](https://suspecframework.dev/skills/): The product — the methodology skill family plus universal disciplines, installed globally.
- [suspec-skills writing guide](https://suspecframework.dev/skills/writing/): How Suspec skills are written, scoped, and reviewed.
- [suspec-cli](https://suspecframework.dev/cli/): Optional reinforcement — `suspec check`, the deterministic honesty floor; exit codes 0/1/2.
- [suspec-mcp](https://suspecframework.dev/mcp/): The same check surface for shell-less MCP runners — two tools, `suspec_check_file` and `suspec_get_checks`.
- [suspec-agents](https://suspecframework.dev/agents/): Optional Claude Code worker definitions — reviewer, auditor, spec author, challenger, researcher, documentarian.

## Source

- [Canon on GitHub](https://github.com/jcosta33/suspec): The source repository for the docs, ADRs, and the checks contract.
- [Skill catalog](https://github.com/jcosta33/suspec-skills): The installable skill family.
- [Website source](https://github.com/jcosta33/suspec-website): The public website repository.
